events.label: assistant
events.text:
I've assessed the kinked/flattened EMT bend and written the judgment to `/app/answer.json`.

**Conclusion: fail, medium risk.** A kinked/flattened bend effectively reduces the internal diameter of the EMT (estimated ~20–30% at the deformed point), which directly violates NEC 358.24 ("the internal diameter of the tubing will not be effectively reduced"). This concentrates conductor fill beyond Chapter 9 Table 4 allowances at that point and risks insulation damage when conductors are pulled through the deformity. Since the run is rough-complete but not yet pulled or closed in, the corrective action is straightforward: cut out and replace the damaged section with a properly bent piece (correct bender, minimum radius per Ch. 9 Table 2), mandrel-check it, and re-request inspection before covering.
